title: 180° Rotation About the Origin Rule
description: Apply the coordinate rule for rotating a point 180° about the origin.
condition: A point $(x, y)$ is rotated $180^\circ$ about the origin.
response: Compute the image as $(-x, -y)$.
label: Rule
example: Rotate $(s, t)$ by $180^\circ$ about the origin to get $(-s, -t)$.
notes: Equivalent to reflecting across both axes.

title: 90° Clockwise Rotation Rule
description: Apply the coordinate rule for rotating a point 90° clockwise about the origin.
condition: A point $(x, y)$ is rotated $90^\circ$ clockwise about the origin.
response: Compute the image as $(y, -x)$.
label: Rule
example: Rotate $(2, 5)$ by $90^\circ$ clockwise to get $(5, -2)$.
notes: Used to distinguish from counterclockwise rotation.

title: Alternate Interior Angles Are Congruent
description: When two parallel lines are cut by a transversal, alternate interior angles are congruent.
condition: Two parallel lines cut by a transversal; angles identified as alternate interior.
response: Set the measures of the two angles equal.
label: Principle / Model
example: If lines ℓ ∥ m and ∠1 and ∠2 are alternate interior, then m∠1 = m∠2.

title: Apply the Pythagorean Theorem
description: Use the Pythagorean Theorem to find a missing side length in a right triangle.
condition: A right triangle with legs a and b and hypotenuse c, with two side lengths known.
response: Substitute into a^2 + b^2 = c^2 (or rearranged form) and solve for the unknown side.
label: Principle / Model
example: If a = 6 and b = 11, then c = \sqrt{6^2 + 11^2} = \sqrt{36 + 121} = \sqrt{157}.

title: Area Rearrangement Argument for Pythagorean Theorem
description: Use rearrangement of congruent triangles to argue that remaining areas are equal, establishing c^2 = a^2 + b^2.
condition: Two composite figures formed from the same set of triangles arranged differently.
response: Reason that since the triangles have equal total area in both figures, the remaining areas must be equal.
label: Principle / Model
example: If one arrangement leaves a square of area c^2 and another leaves squares of areas a^2 and b^2, then c^2 = a^2 + b^2.

title: Compose Rigid Motions in Given Order
description: Apply multiple rigid motions in the specified sequence to determine the final image.
condition: Given a sequence such as rotation followed by reflection.
response: Apply each transformation in order to determine the resulting figure or coordinates.
label: Rule
example: Rotate $(2,1)$ 90° CCW to $(-1,2)$, then reflect across the y-axis to $(1,2)$.

title: Compute Space Diagonal of Rectangular Prism
description: Find the length of the space diagonal of a rectangular prism using the Pythagorean Theorem twice or the 3D formula.
condition: Given length l, width w, and height h of a right rectangular prism and asked for the space diagonal.
response: Compute $d = \sqrt{l^2 + w^2 + h^2}$ (or first find base diagonal, then apply Pythagorean Theorem with height).
label: Principle / Model
notes: Extends Pythagorean Theorem to three dimensions.

title: Congruence via Rigid Motions
description: Two figures are congruent if a sequence of rigid motions maps one onto the other.
condition: Two geometric figures in the plane.
response: Determine whether a sequence of translations, rotations, or reflections maps one figure exactly onto the other.
label: Principle / Model
example: Reflect △ABC across x-axis, then translate to coincide with △FGH, proving congruence.

title: Corresponding Angles Are Congruent
description: When two parallel lines are cut by a transversal, corresponding angles are congruent.
condition: Two parallel lines cut by a transversal; angles identified as corresponding.
response: Set the measures of the two angles equal.
label: Principle / Model
example: If lines ℓ ∥ m and ∠1 corresponds to ∠2, then m∠1 = m∠2.
notes: Used to equate expressions such as 3x + 15 and 78.

title: Distance Formula in Coordinate Plane
description: Find the distance between two points using the distance formula derived from the Pythagorean Theorem.
condition: Given two points (x1, y1) and (x2, y2) in the coordinate plane.
response: Compute $d = \sqrt{(x2 - x1)^2 + (y2 - y1)^2}$ and simplify or approximate.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Application of Pythagorean Theorem to coordinate geometry.

title: Exterior Angle Theorem
description: In a triangle, an exterior angle equals the sum of the two remote interior angles.
condition: A triangle with an exterior angle formed by extending one side.
response: Set exterior angle measure equal to sum of the two remote interior angles.
label: Principle / Model
example: If an exterior angle is (4x + 3)° and remote interior angles are 48° and 87°, then 4x + 3 = 48 + 87.
notes: Explains equation setup in triangle problems.

title: Identify Hypotenuse as Longest Side
description: Recognize that in a right triangle, the hypotenuse is the longest side.
condition: Given side lengths of a triangle and determining which side would be the hypotenuse if the triangle is right.
response: Select the longest side as the hypotenuse candidate.
label: Concept
example: For sides 3, 5, 6, the longest side is 6, so 6 would be the hypotenuse candidate.

title: Identify Non-Congruent Figures
description: Determine that two figures are not congruent when no sequence of rigid motions maps one onto the other.
condition: Given two plane figures that differ in side lengths or angle measures under all rigid motions.
response: State that the figures are not congruent.
label: Concept
example: If one triangle has side lengths $3,4,5$ and another has $3,4,6$, they are not congruent.

title: Inverse of 90° Rotation
description: Recognize that a 90° clockwise rotation is the inverse of a 90° counterclockwise rotation about the same point.
condition: Given a figure rotated 90° counterclockwise about the origin and asked how to map it back.
response: Apply a 90° clockwise rotation about the same point.
label: Rule
example: If $(x,y)$ is mapped to $(-y,x)$ by a 90° CCW rotation, then applying $(y,-x)$ (90° CW) returns it to $(x,y)$.

title: Linear Pair Sum to 180°
description: When two angles form a linear pair on a straight line, their measures sum to 180°.
condition: Two adjacent angles whose noncommon sides form a straight line.
response: Set up equation m∠a + m∠b = 180°.
label: Principle / Model
example: If m∠A = 110° and ∠A and ∠B form a linear pair, then m∠B = 180° - 110° = 70°.
notes: Used when angles lie on a straight line.

title: Parallelism Preserved Under Translation
description: Recognize that if two segments are parallel, their images under a translation are also parallel.
condition: A translation is applied to a figure with two parallel segments.
response: Conclude that the corresponding image segments remain parallel.
label: Concept
example: If $\overline{WX} \parallel \overline{ZY}$, then after a translation, $\overline{W'X'} \parallel \overline{Z'Y'}$.

title: Reflect Across the x-Axis
description: A reflection across the x-axis maps each point to a point with the same x-coordinate and opposite y-coordinate.
condition: Point $(x,y)$ reflected across the x-axis.
response: Map to $(x,-y)$.
label: Principle / Model
example: $(2,-3)$ reflected across x-axis becomes $(2,3)$.

title: Reflection Across the y-Axis Rule
description: Apply the coordinate rule for reflecting a point across the y-axis.
condition: A point (x, y) reflected across the y-axis.
response: Map to (-x, y).
label: Rule / Plan
example: Reflect (4, -2) across the y-axis to get (-4, -2).

title: Rigid Motion Maps Corresponding Angles
description: If a rigid motion (translation or rotation) maps one angle onto another, then the angles are congruent.
condition: A specified rigid motion maps angle A onto angle B.
response: Conclude m∠A = m∠B.
label: Concept
example: If a translation along \(\overrightarrow{PR}\) maps ∠1 onto ∠3, then m∠1 = m∠3.
notes: Response is always that the angles are congruent.

title: Rigid Motions Preserve Length and Angle Measure
description: Use the property that reflections, rotations, and translations preserve distances and angle measures.
condition: Given a figure and its image under a rigid motion.
response: Conclude that corresponding side lengths and angle measures are equal.
label: Principle
example: If $\angle H = 135^\circ$ in a figure, then after a reflection and rotation, $\angle H'' = 135^\circ$.

title: Rotate 90° Counterclockwise About Origin
description: Apply the coordinate rule for a 90° counterclockwise rotation about the origin.
condition: Given a point $(x,y)$ rotated 90° counterclockwise about the origin.
response: Produce the image point $(-y,x)$.
label: Principle
example: Rotating $(3,5)$ 90° CCW about the origin gives $(-5,3)$.

title: Round to Nearest Tenth
description: Round a decimal value to the nearest tenth based on the hundredths digit.
condition: Given a decimal number and instructed to round to the nearest tenth.
response: Check the hundredths digit and round the tenths digit up or down accordingly.
label: Rule / Plan
example: $6.73$ rounded to the nearest tenth is $6.7$ because the hundredths digit (3) is less than 5.

title: Solve Linear Equation in One Variable
description: Solve a linear equation by isolating the variable using inverse operations.
condition: An equation of the form ax + b = c.
response: Subtract b from both sides and divide by a to find x.
label: Principle / Model
notes: [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Substitute Algebraic Angle Expression
description: Replace an angle measure in a geometric relationship with its algebraic expression.
condition: An angle measure is given as an algebraic expression (e.g., 3x + 15).
response: Insert the expression into the relevant angle equation.
label: Rule / Plan
example: If m∠1 = 3x + 15 and corresponding angles are equal to 78°, write 3x + 15 = 78.

title: Translation on Coordinate Plane
description: A translation moves every point by the same horizontal and/or vertical distance.
condition: Point $(x,y)$ translated by $(a,b)$.
response: Map to $(x+a, y+b)$.
label: Principle / Model
example: Translate $(1,2)$ 6 units right → $(7,2)$.

title: Translations Preserve Length and Angle
description: Understand that translations preserve distances and angle measures.
condition: A figure is mapped under a translation and a length or angle measure is questioned.
response: State that the corresponding image length or angle measure is unchanged.
label: Concept
example: If $\angle WXY = 90^\circ$, then after translation $\angle W'X'Y' = 90^\circ$, and if $XY = 4$, then $X'Y' = 4$.

title: Triangle Angle Sum Theorem
description: Use the fact that the sum of the interior angles of a triangle is 180 degrees.
condition: A triangle with two interior angles known.
response: Set up equation angle1 + angle2 + angle3 = 180 and solve for the unknown angle.
label: Principle / Model
example: If angles are 71^\circ and 28^\circ, then the third angle is 180 - 71 - 28 = 81^\circ.

title: Verify Right Triangle Using Converse
description: Determine whether a triangle is right by checking if its side lengths satisfy the Pythagorean Theorem.
condition: Given three side lengths a, b, c of a triangle.
response: Check whether the square of the longest side equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides; conclude right or not right.
label: Principle / Model
example: For sides 20, 21, 29: $20^2+21^2=400+441=841$ and $29^2=841$, so the triangle is right.
